gu-v-fogler-rubinoff-2026-01-23.lesson: When opposing a pro-se filer, check every citation yourself—courts reward counsel who surface AI hallucinations.

gonen-v-ashkenazi-2026-01-22.lesson: A dismissed claim tied to fake citations can end the matter—verify every authority before filing, even in small-claims forums.

cruz-v-manzano-y-asociados-2026-01-21.lesson: Monetary sanctions now cross borders—require a second-attorney cite-check before every filing, no exceptions.

zou-v-miracon-development-inc-2026-01-20.lesson: Even token costs against pro-se filers set precedent—cite these in motions to deter opposing AI misuse.

waddell-re-2026-01-20.lesson: Facta without hyperlinks or reporter cites now trigger AI-hallucination suspicion—include verifiable links on every authority.

pellegrino-v-york-condominium-corporatio.lesson: Tribunals are now pre-warning fabricators that future hallucinations will draw costs—cite that warning in your own briefs.

the-republic-of-uganda-v-tugume-abubakar.lesson: AI hallucinations are a global lawyer problem—verify every Commonwealth citation against the actual reporter before filing.

amit-arora-v-canadian-national-railway-2.lesson: Federal courts now catch hallucinated citations and misstated rules—build checklists that flag both fake cites and fake legal propositions.

riley-v-nuvei-australia-merchant-service.lesson: Commissions flag fabricated authorities even without sanctions—log these dismissals to train opposing-counsel detection.

your-home-partners-v-kellichan-2026-01-1.lesson: Fabricated statutes are now appearing alongside fake cases—verify the existence of the statute section, not just the case number.

robert-huish-v-hrmc-2026-01-16.lesson: When a filer admits AI use after being caught, push the tribunal to disregard the entire filing, not just the fake cites.

lutfi-v-tucker-2026-01-16.lesson: Family-court self-reps are a major AI-hallucination vector—request authority-by-authority verification orders up front.

case-no-2025가단214167-2026-01-13.lesson: Korean courts are now tracking AI hallucinations—international clients should assume no jurisdiction is safe for unverified cites.

smith-and-commissioner-of-taxation-2026-.lesson: AI also misapplies real cases—verify both that the cite exists and that it actually supports the proposition claimed.

vanderberg-v-shviro-2026-01-12.lesson: Small-claims fines for fake cites are now standardized globally—expect monetary consequences even in low-value matters.

vasina-v-york-condominium-corporation-no.lesson: Condo tribunals are seeing repeat AI hallucination patterns—flag prior fabricator filings when you spot the same filer.

ms-f-green-v-imprint-creative-print-solu.lesson: Employment tribunals now disregard AI-sourced authorities wholesale—challenge the entire argument, not just the cites.

alami-nature-electricity-ltd-v-nof-ganus.lesson: Winning on the merits no longer guarantees costs—fake cites can strip a prevailing party's fee award.

alexandra-philomena-brigid-roy-v-attorne.lesson: Federal Court now routinely disregards unreliable citations—attack opponent's authorities as a threshold matter.

esteme-v-aquitaine-co-investment-2026-01.lesson: Allegations of 14 fake cites in one brief signal systemic AI reliance—audit counsel's entire practice, not just the filing.
